attacking_style: Pure sprinter who excels at self-positioning in final 2km, often switching sides of road to find clean path. Lacks explosive final 50m acceleration against top-tier sprinters despite excellent setup.
team_tactics: Frequently operates without dedicated leadout, forced to self-position from 2km out. When team present, receives standard final-kilometer pacing but often becomes tactically isolated in crucial final 300m.
terrain_preference: Flat bunch sprints with technical finales requiring positioning intelligence. Vulnerable on circuits with repeated climbs (4+ ascents) where cannot maintain contact despite pure sprint speed.
vulnerability: Consistently loses photo finishes by inches despite perfect positioning—timing deficiency in final 50m. Cannot follow Philipsen-caliber acceleration. Exposed on circuit races with climbing repetition and crash-prone technical finishes.
key_patterns: Three consecutive Tour de France podiums but beaten in photo finish each time—positioning excellent, timing deficient (2023)
Regularly leads own sprint without team support from 2km, showing independent tactical ability but isolation (4 instances)
Switches road position late when boxed, exploiting spatial awareness to salvage results (3 documented instances)
Resilient recovery capacity: won sprint after early crash, raced Dauphine two days post-Giro (2 instances)
Second-place specialist against top sprinters—lacks final kick but consistent top-3 finisher (6 podiums observed)
Team fragmentation pattern: domestiques drop or mistime leadout in technical finales, forcing solo effort (4 races)
form_trajectory: neutral: 2023 TDF showed peak positioning skill with three straight podiums, but raw speed deficit versus Philipsen unchanged. Consistent top-tier results without breakthrough improvement.
